Insights

Insights
Managing money
across multiple FIs
How people stay on top of managing money across multiple FIs, important
moments, and key pain points
Pg 20

Watch - out Managing accounts
The understanding that Canadians
have 2 FIs may not be true
Surveys find that Canadians on average have 2 FIs1.
Many of our participants under -reported how many FIs they deal with. We
suspect Canadians have a specific mental model of what counts as a financial
institution that doesn't include, for example, PC Financial. We noticed that people
don't report having a PC Optimum card until we press for more details.
The typical Canadian customer may not be sharing their complete financial
picture with us. As we continue to plan for an Open Banking future, we may
uncover more diversification than Canadians are currently reporting.
1Online Survey on Financial Data Sharing and Financial Security (Government of Canada, 2021)
Pg 21

Getting comfortable Adoption patterns
These are the activities and rituals that help people decide
whether to share their data using a service or technology:
Performing a lower stakes activity
"I did go through the process [moving money from CIBC to Tangerine] a couple of times without - Key Segments
entering fake information to see, OK, well, what do they need? And then becoming comfortable with
that process before I said, OK, go." –P4, 49 yo, Canada MA
"[Apple Wallet] For me, I just caved in at some point. I put in my husband's card and it was so easy. I
know that doesn't make sense because it's all joint accounts, but some things just make you In this round of research, we did not observe a
[comfortable]." –P4, 38 yo, Canada MM generational difference in which participants
were more vs. less risk -taking.
Doing the research
"Usually I'll check the reviews. Or, there might be stuff that I heard… about different companies, if
I've heard something bad about them or concerns about security or whatever, then maybe I wouldn't
share." –P4, 49 yo, Canada MA
"[do you remember how you got on board with Google home?] I think just reading, hearing people's
stories. I'm on most of the forum boards, I'm a huge Reddit guy… I think the confidence comes from,
what's the fall-back plan? What happens if I get breached? So I look at the whole picture to sort of
get that confidence in it. Once I have it, the only other thing that can shake it is if something happens
down the road." –P16, 42 yo, Canada MM

Protections from fraud and theft Data sharing
When it comes to sharing financial data, people take
additional precautions
Being selective about the company
I would be more inclined to share my data with the big [companies]… I'd probably go with the
more popular name brands, for example Apple, Samsung, as opposed to [another] wallet I don't Key Segments
know anything about. I've used it, I trust the technology, probably the less popular one or more used
ones I'd probably go with the one I know more." –P3, 38 yo, Canada MM
Being selective about what data to share
"With Amazon [I would share] nothing. Just my credit card and address, because I am only buying
In this round of research we did not observe a
from them, they don't need to know anything else about me … Facebook, same things. It's just
generational difference in how people protect
general information. I don't think it even has my birthday." –P4, 49 yo, Canada MA
themselves online while sharing financial data.
Adding only one card to a website

when there's an issue." –P5, 54 yo, Canada MA
Manual entry helps them steer clear of fraud

remove the card… just to make sure, if my card is there, there won't be any fraud transaction. " –P5,

Insight Sharing with banks
None of our participants had heard of
Open Banking before
People don't know about the good or the bad of Open Banking. If we want
customers to understand the difference between screen scraping and sharing via
APIs, we will need to educate them.
Consumer -directed finance might not be a reality until we educate the public.
People won't realize the full potential of Open Banking to help them take charge of
their data unless we equip them to

Key Segments
relax." –P1, 22, Canada MM
People feel comfortable when there is some separation between institutions

either, but I don't have control over that. This I have control over." –P4, 49 yo, Canada MA a future with Open Banking.

User Interviews
A form of data collection that allows researchers to ask people open - Key Characteristics
ended questions about their experiences.
• They are flexible and iterative in nature, and are best suited for
exploratory research questions .
• Offers deep exploration of participant thoughts and experiences,
as they are given the space to express their opinions, ideas,
feelings, and attitudes.
• Gathers information from people who can provide insight on a
particular topic.
• They often involve questions (or conversational exercises) that
allow for participants to express their thoughts and feelings,
while addressing the study’s topic or area of focus.
Sparkes & Smith, 2014; Smith & Sparkes, 2019
Humans are conversational creatures, and through conversation we
can get to know other people. Participants can reveal much more
about the meanings they attach to their experiences, providing the
interviewer with deeper knowledge than can be gleaned through
other methods such as surveys.
Sources: Sparkes & Smith, 2014; Smith & Sparkes, 2019 Pg 86
